column4: Slave-substitution template (shared w/ Sniper Rifle.4/R1669): effects scoped to "the acting minion" don't extend to a Slave standing in for it
combat card usable only in combat restates the obvious combat-timing rule; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 5.5 (Satyr's as-a-vampire ability) but teaches nothing about that mechanism specifically
!TENSION:no-effect-plays dodge is not prevented, it just accomplishes nothing — same question as Draba.3, opposite mechanism
bare damage-type classification fact about one card; no interaction or consequence stated. Fails transfer — doesn't inform another card's ruling
!TENSION:no-effect-plays destroy-equipment card requires equipment to exist; prohibition-side parallel to the prevention-card template below
clarifies scope of this card's own rearrange effect (which item types are covered); doesn't inform other cards' effect scopes. Nearest 1.12 (cards on cards)
numeric/no-limit fact specific to this card's own storage capacity; no transfer to other storage cards, which print their own limits. Nearest 1.14
!TENSION:no-effect-plays prevention card requires unresolved damage to be playable at all
pure card-vs-card fact ({Unleash Hell's Fury} vs this card's bearer); fails transfer — concerns only these two named cards. Nearest 3.3 (who may block)
!TENSION:no-effect-plays a zero-blood vampire remains a valid target even though the action may accomplish nothing
!TENSION:no-effect-plays same prevention-requires-damage template as R1634
!TENSION:no-effect-plays same template
Slave-substitution template, shared w/ R1599
!TENSION:no-effect-plays same template
!TENSION:no-effect-plays matches calibration's Blood Fury.6 exactly (opponent barred from playing prevention cards even for zero effect)
!DROPPED:5.5
card-vs-card exemption fact ({Wormwood} doesn't affect these tokens); fails transfer — concerns only this pairing. Nearest 1.4 (representation)
narrow non-contest fact between named variant printings of one card family; fails transfer beyond this small family. Nearest 4.5 (prevention effects usable together)

minion-phase action ordering — the order a Methuselah takes their own actions (mandatory before non-mandatory) has no owning section; 2.4 is simultaneous-effect ordering, a different thing. This is the taxonomy's own cited Spirit Marionette watch-list example
!TENSION:no-effect-plays mandatory bonus cannot apply to an already-unsuccessful/zero bleed — same shape as the prevention-card template

multiple copies of the same mandatory-trigger effect don't all fire once the shared triggering state changes after the first — no section owns multi-copy trigger interaction; nearest 1.2 (periodicity), same watch-list gap family as Betrayer.3/Camarilla Exemplary.1 cumulative/stacking
!DROPPED:3.4
card-specific crypt-retrieval pairing rule (Aye/Orun) unique to this Laibon mechanic; fails transfer to other retrieval effects. Nearest 6.7

sect as a trait (watch-list) — being non-Anarch doesn't block equipping an Anarch-flavored card, only suppresses the effects that require it; nothing owns sect itself as a gating trait (cf. taxonomy's Galaric's Legacy watch-list entry). 1.6 nearest (requirements to play vs. keep)
same sect-as-trait gap — equipping an Anarch-flavored card is not itself "an action requiring an Anarch." 3.7.3 nearest (equip actions) but doesn't own sect-as-trait
!TENSION:no-effect-plays same prevention-requires-damage template
!DROPPED:1.9
